Protein names: Bacteriochlorophyllide d C-20 methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.333) (Bacteriochlorophyllide d MTase)
Organism: Chlorobaculum tepidum (strain ATCC 49652 / DSM 12025 / NBRC 103806 / TLS) (Chlorobium tepidum)
Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a bacteriochlorophyllide d + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a bacteriochlorophyllide c + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:49180, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:90955, ChEBI:CHEBI:90965; EC=2.1.1.333; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:15090495, ECO:0000305|PubMed:16797589};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of the major light-harvesting pigment bacteriochlorophyll c (BChlc), which confers a significant competitive advantage to green sulfur bacteria living at limiting red and near-infrared light intensities (PubMed:15090495). Catalyzes the methylation at the C-20 position of the cyclic tetrapyrrole chlorin of bacteriochlorophyll d (BChld) to produce bacteriochlorophyll c (BChlc) using S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) as a methyl source (PubMed:15090495, PubMed:16797589). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:15090495, ECO:0000269|PubMed:16797589}.
